from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural language description from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping predicates to phrase generators
    phrasing = {
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is also known as {o}",
        "creator": lambda s, o: f"{s} was created by {o}",
        "fullName": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s full name is {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded by {o}",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"{s} has key person {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "product": lambda s, o: f"{s} produces {o}",
        "lastAired": lambda s, o: f"{s} last aired on {o}",
        "starring": lambda s, o: f"{s} stars {o}",
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": lambda s, o: f"{s} first appeared in the film {o}",
        "voice": lambda s, o: f"{s} is voiced by {o}",
        "distributor": lambda s, o: f"{o} distributed {s}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o}",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the {o}",
        "broadcastedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} was broadcasted by {o}",
        "firstAired": lambda s, o: f"{s} first aired on {o}",
        "series": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the series {o}",
        "child": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a child named {o}",
    }
    # Helper to clean surrounding quotes from objects
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        return obj.strip('"')

    # Group predicates by subject to create compact clauses
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_clauses = defaultdict(list)

    for triple in triples:
        func = phrasing.get(triple.predicate)
        obj = _clean(triple.object)
        if func:
            full_phrase = func(triple.subject, obj)
        else:
            full_phrase = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {obj}"
        # Store the full phrase; later we will strip repeated subject
        subject_clauses[triple.subject].append(full_phrase)

    # Build a single fluent sentence from grouped clauses
    combined_parts = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_clauses.items():
        if not phrases:
            continue
        # First phrase keeps the subject
        first = phrases[0]
        # Subsequent phrases drop the leading subject
        rest = [p[len(subj):].lstrip() for p in phrases[1:]]
        if rest:
            # Join rest with commas and final 'and'
            if len(rest) == 1:
                joined_rest = rest[0]
            else:
                joined_rest = ", ".join(rest[:-1]) + f", and {rest[-1]}"
            clause = f"{first}, {joined_rest}"
        else:
            clause = first
        combined_parts.append(clause)

    # Join different subject clauses with '; '
    combined = "; ".join(combined_parts)

    # Ensure the sentence ends with a single period.
    return combined.rstrip() + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
